evals.prompt: Start the server
evals.expected_output: Builds project, checks port, starts server in subagent/background, verifies with curl

evals.prompt: Restart the server, I think it's stale
evals.expected_output: Kills existing process, rebuilds, starts fresh in subagent/background, verifies

evals.prompt: Take a screenshot of the homepage
evals.expected_output: Detects server needed, builds, starts in subagent, verifies, then takes screenshot

evals.prompt: Open the app in the browser and show me the settings page
evals.expected_output: Ensures server is running in subagent, navigates browser to settings page

evals.prompt: Take screenshots of every page and save them to screenshots/
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, then systematically screenshots each route

evals.prompt: Run the E2E tests
evals.expected_output: Builds, starts server in subagent, then runs E2E test suite

evals.prompt: Check the end-to-end flow of creating a task in the browser
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, opens browser, walks through task creation flow step by step

evals.prompt: Verify the login flow works after my auth changes
evals.expected_output: Rebuilds with latest changes, starts server in subagent, tests login flow in browser

evals.prompt: Check if the /api/tasks endpoint returns data
evals.expected_output: Ensures server running in subagent, curls the endpoint, shows response

evals.prompt: Hit the /api/health endpoint and show me the response
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent if needed, curls health endpoint, displays result

evals.prompt: Audit the UI/UX flow for a new user signing up
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, opens browser, walks through signup flow noting UX issues

evals.prompt: Walk through the app and find any broken links or UI issues
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, systematically navigates pages looking for broken elements

evals.prompt: Check if the mobile layout looks right on the dashboard
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, resizes browser to mobile viewport, screenshots dashboard

evals.prompt: The tests are failing with connection refused
evals.expected_output: Recognizes server is probably down, starts it in subagent, reruns tests

evals.prompt: I updated the database schema, can you check the settings page looks right?
evals.expected_output: Rebuilds to pick up schema changes, starts server in subagent, checks settings page

evals.prompt: Compare the current UI with this Figma design
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent, takes screenshot of current UI for comparison

evals.prompt: Fix the typo in the README
evals.expected_output: Edits README directly, no server needed

evals.prompt: Refactor the database query in queries.ts
evals.expected_output: Code refactoring only, no server needed

evals.prompt: What does the server-manager skill do?
evals.expected_output: Explains the skill, does not start a server

evals.prompt: Write unit tests for the utility functions
evals.expected_output: Writes and runs unit tests directly, no server needed

evals.prompt: Take a screenshot of the board — I just checked it manually and it's working fine
evals.expected_output: Checks port to confirm server is healthy, skips rebuild/restart since user confirmed it's working, proceeds to screenshot

evals.prompt: Spin up the dev environment and check the API responses
evals.expected_output: Detects docker-compose.yml if present, uses docker compose up -d, otherwise falls back to normal start flow in subagent

evals.prompt: I just pushed a big refactor, can you open the app and make sure nothing looks broken?
evals.expected_output: Rebuilds to pick up refactor changes, starts server in subagent, visually inspects key pages

evals.prompt: Test the WebSocket connection on the board page
evals.expected_output: Starts server in subagent (noting multi-port setup if applicable), opens browser, tests WebSocket connectivity

evals.prompt: Check localhost:3000 and tell me what you see
evals.expected_output: Checks if something is on port 3000, if not checks project config for actual port, starts server on correct port in subagent

evals.prompt: Add a new API route to handle user deletion
evals.expected_output: Writes the route code, no server needed — this is implementation, not testing

evals.prompt: Review the server startup logic in src/cli/up.ts
evals.expected_output: Reads and explains the code — mentions 'server' but is just code review, no server needed

evals.prompt: Run npm test
evals.expected_output: Runs unit tests directly — npm test is not E2E, no server needed

evals.prompt: Update the server port configuration in the config file
evals.expected_output: Edits config file — mentions 'server' and 'port' but is just a file edit, no server needed

evals.prompt: Debug why the server crashes on startup — look at the error in the logs
evals.expected_output: Reads log files and code to diagnose — mentions 'server' but is debugging, not starting one
